# Provider Keycloak

> Crossplane provider for declarative Keycloak management via Kubernetes custom resources.

provider-keycloak is a Crossplane provider that lets you manage Keycloak resources (realms, clients, users, roles, groups, protocol mappers, identity providers, user federation) as Kubernetes custom resources. It continuously reconciles desired state with actual Keycloak state.

## Project Information

- Repository: https://github.com/crossplane-contrib/provider-keycloak
- Documentation: https://crossplane-contrib.github.io/provider-keycloak/
- Releases: https://github.com/crossplane-contrib/provider-keycloak/releases
- Built with: Upjet (https://github.com/crossplane/upjet) + Keycloak Terraform Provider

## Core Concepts

- **ProviderConfig**: Connection details for a Keycloak instance (URL, credentials)
- **Managed Resources**: Kubernetes CRDs that map 1:1 to Keycloak objects
- **Reconciliation**: Controller continuously ensures Keycloak matches desired state
- **Drift Detection**: Changes made outside the provider are detected and corrected

## API Groups

- `keycloak.crossplane.io` - ProviderConfig
- `realm.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Realms, RealmEvents, RequiredAction, UserProfile, KeystoreRsa, DefaultClientScopes, OptionalClientScopes, ClientPolicyProfile, ClientPolicyProfilePolicy
- `openidclient.keycloak.crossplane.io` - OpenID Connect Clients, ClientScopes, ClientDefaultScopes, ClientOptionalScopes, ClientPermissions, ClientAuthorizationResource, ClientAuthorizationPermission, ClientClientPolicy, ClientGroupPolicy, ClientRolePolicy, ClientUserPolicy, ClientRegexPolicy, ClientServiceAccountRealmRole, ClientServiceAccountRole
- `user.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Users, UserGroups, UserRoles, UserPermissions, CustomUserFederation
- `role.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Roles
- `group.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Groups, Memberships, GroupRoles, GroupPermissions
- `client.keycloak.crossplane.io` - ProtocolMapper, RoleMapper
- `openidgroup.keycloak.crossplane.io` - GroupMembershipProtocolMapper
- `oidc.keycloak.crossplane.io` - OIDC IdentityProvider, GoogleIdentityProvider
- `saml.keycloak.crossplane.io` - SAML IdentityProvider
- `identityprovider.keycloak.crossplane.io` - IdentityProviderMapper, KubernetesIdentityProvider, OidcOpenShiftV4IdentityProvider, SpiffeIdentityProvider, ProviderTokenExchangeScopePermission
- `ldap.keycloak.crossplane.io` - UserFederation, UserAttributeMapper, FullNameMapper, GroupMapper, RoleMapper, HardcodedAttributeMapper, HardcodedGroupMapper, HardcodedRoleMapper, MsadUserAccountControlMapper, MsadLdsUserAccountControlMapper, CustomMapper
- `samlclient.keycloak.crossplane.io` - SAML Client, ClientScope, ClientDefaultScopes
- `defaults.keycloak.crossplane.io` - DefaultGroups, DefaultRoles
- `authenticationflow.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Flow, Subflow, Execution, ExecutionConfig, Bindings
- `organization.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Organization
- `workflow.keycloak.crossplane.io` - Workflow

## Installation

```yaml
apiVersion: pkg.crossplane.io/v1
kind: Provider
metadata:
  name: provider-keycloak
spec:
  package: xpkg.upbound.io/crossplane-contrib/provider-keycloak:<version>
```

## Configuration

Create a Secret with Keycloak credentials and a ProviderConfig referencing it:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: keycloak-credentials
  namespace: crossplane-system
type: Opaque
stringData:
  credentials: |
    {
      "client_id": "admin-cli",
      "username": "admin",
      "password": "admin",
      "url": "https://keycloak.example.com",
      "base_path": "/auth",
      "realm": "master"
    }
---
apiVersion: keycloak.crossplane.io/v1beta1
kind: ProviderConfig
metadata:
  name: keycloak-provider-config
spec:
  credentials:
    source: Secret
    secretRef:
      name: keycloak-credentials
      key: credentials
      namespace: crossplane-system
```

## Credential Fields

- url (required): Keycloak server URL
- client_id (required): OAuth2 client ID
- username: Admin username (for password grant)
- password: Admin password (for password grant)
- client_secret: Client secret (for client credentials grant)
- realm: Authentication realm (defaults to "master")
- base_path: URL path prefix (e.g., "/auth" for pre-v17 Keycloak)
- root_ca_certificate: Custom CA certificate for TLS

## Resource Examples

### Realm
```yaml
apiVersion: realm.keycloak.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Realm
metadata:
  name: my-realm
spec:
  forProvider:
    realm: "my-realm"
    enabled: true
  providerConfigRef:
    name: keycloak-provider-config
```

### Client (OIDC)
```yaml
apiVersion: openidclient.keycloak.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Client
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  forProvider:
    clientId: my-app
    realmId: my-realm
    accessType: public
    standardFlowEnabled: true
    validRedirectUris:
      - "http://localhost:3000/callback"
  providerConfigRef:
    name: keycloak-provider-config
```

### User
```yaml
apiVersion: user.keycloak.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: User
metadata:
  name: demo-user
spec:
  forProvider:
    realmId: "my-realm"
    username: "demo"
    email: "demo@example.com"
    enabled: true
  providerConfigRef:
    name: keycloak-provider-config
```

### Role
```yaml
apiVersion: role.keycloak.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: admin-role
spec:
  forProvider:
    realmId: "my-realm"
    name: "admin"
  providerConfigRef:
    name: keycloak-provider-config
```

### Group
```yaml
apiVersion: group.keycloak.crossplane.io/v1alpha1
kind: Group
metadata:
  name: developers
spec:
  forProvider:
    name: "Developers"
    realmId: "my-realm"
```
